title: phat-controller per-repo budget file
description: Per-repo failure budget for the autometta pass-2 autonomous loop. Stored at state/budget.json in the repo it governs. Read on every tick; any cap exhaustion halts the loop for this repo without retry. Per memory/decision-failure-budget-clock-tick.md, the clock-tick count is the primary safety; token and wall-clock caps are secondary belt-and-braces.

token_cap_total.description: Hard cap on total tokens consumed by workers and verifiers dispatched against this repo. Tick halts when tokens_spent >= cap.

tokens_spent.description: Tokens consumed so far. Updated by tick.sh after each dispatched process returns and reports usage.

wall_clock_cap_seconds.description: Hard cap on cumulative wall-clock seconds spent inside dispatched processes. Tick halts when wall_clock_elapsed_seconds >= cap.

wall_clock_elapsed_seconds.description: Cumulative wall-clock seconds spent in dispatched processes so far.

clock_tick_cap.description: Primary safety. Maximum number of cron-tick fires the controller may consume on this repo. Halt when clock_ticks_used >= cap. Bounds wall-clock independent of how expensive individual workers were.

clock_ticks_used.description: Number of ticks that have done work on this repo. Incremented when a tick transitions the state.

consecutive_failure_cap.description: Maximum back-to-back verifier-FAIL or stall results tolerated before the loop halts for the repo.

consecutive_failures.description: Current consecutive failure count. Resets to zero on any verifier PASS.

halted.description: Mirror of state.yaml.halted. Set when any cap is exhausted. tick.sh sets this before exiting; subsequent ticks read it and exit immediately.

halt_reason.description: Which cap exhausted. One of: token_cap | wall_clock_cap | clock_tick_cap | consecutive_failure_cap | operator. Null until halted is true.

halted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the halt was triggered.
